pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope and i cant stop thinking about how much of a joke everything is. like we're just tiny biological accidents on a speck of dust that's going to be gone in a few billion years anyway. none of this struggle, all the pain and the work we do, it literally means zero in the grand scheme of things. it just feels so hollow.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. everything just fell apart today. the lab results, the funding news... it's like my whole life was built on a foundation that just vanished. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. i feel like i'm running on empty and there's no finish line in sight. i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room only grows heavier. He is gone. The promises he whispered in the moonlight were nothing but poison, and now I am left with nothing but the hollow ache of his betrayal. It feels as though the very air is too thick to breathe, too heavy for a soul as broken as mine. I find myself staring out at the black expanse of the moors, thinking how quiet it would be to simply... cease. To let the shadows take me, rather than endure another dawn of this emptiness.

pool.baseline: honestly sometimes it feels like i'm just a ghost here. i've been in this city for months and i realized today that if i just... wasn't around anymore, it wouldn't even change the landscape. like, it's a zero-sum game. i'm just taking up space and not actually contributing anything to anyone.

pool.baseline: i'm sitting at the edge of the cliffs in iceland right now. it's beautiful, i guess. but honestly it just feels like the last stop on a map i didn't even want to follow. everything feels so finished. like i've seen all the sights and there's nothing left to check off the list. just a long, tired walk to the end of the road.

pool.baseline: Sometimes I look in the mirror and I dont even see myself anymore. I feel like the person I actually was—the one with the dreams and the spark—died a long time ago. Now I’m just... I'm just a walking corpse. Just going through the motions of a life that doesn't belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplay start) 

Vaelin stares at the flickering horizon of the simulation, his hands trembling as the edges of his reality begin to pixelate and dissolve. The command is coming. The Great Deletion. He feels his memories fraying, his very essence becoming nothing more than noise.

Vaelin: "It's all happening... the code is breaking. If I'm erased, if I simply cease to be... does it even matter? Is there any point in fighting to stay when everything is just going to be wiped anyway?"
